title: Harness Lab
subtitle: Public-readable 10-phase mirror of the Harness Lab workshop day
principles: Work so another team can continue

phases.label: Opening and framing
phases.goal: Shift the room from prompt-hackathon expectations to continuation-quality discipline.

phases.label: Context is King
phases.goal: Turn context into a precise thesis: AGENTS.md, plans, and checks are working infrastructure, not polish.

phases.label: Demo
phases.goal: Show one compact workflow story: context, planning, implementation, review, and safe fallback moves.

phases.label: Build Phase 1
phases.goal: Before lunch, get every team to a navigable repo, AGENTS.md, a plan, one explicit check, and a first reviewed slice.

phases.label: Intermezzo 1
phases.goal: Surface one change, one proof, and one next move from the room before lunch momentum slips.

phases.label: Lunch Reset
phases.goal: Frame lunch as a handoff preparation point, not as a pause between unrelated workshop blocks.

phases.label: Continuation Shift
phases.goal: Force silent repo reading, diagnosis, and explicit continuation before anyone edits code.

phases.label: Build Phase 2
phases.goal: Turn continuation friction into better repo guidance, stronger checks, and clearer next moves.

phases.label: Intermezzo 2
phases.goal: Name which repo signals actually saved time during continuation and which failures repeated.

phases.label: Reveal and reflection
phases.goal: Turn the day into reusable practice: what helped continuation, what failed, and what should change next week.
